name: Natural Park of Serra d'Irta — Circular from Alcocebre
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Irta — Circular desde Alcocebre
region_intro: Hiking around Alcalà De Xivert offers diverse terrain, blending mountainous landscapes with the Mediterranean coastline in the province of Castellón. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, where mountains descend to the sea, forming steep cliffs and secluded coves. This protected area features dense vegetation, including pine trees and holm oaks, alongside a captivating shoreline with beaches and coves. The varied environment includes plains, mountains, and coastal features, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Alcalà De Xivert The most popular hiking route is Circular Tour to Xivert Castle, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route leads to the ancient Xivert Castle, offering panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast. Another top favourite among local hikers is Natural Park of Serra d'Irta — Circular from Alcocebre, a difficult 17.4 miles (28.0 km) path. This challenging route explores the rugged landscape of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, featuring coastal views and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the View of Ermita de Santa Llúcia – White Cove loop from Alcossebre, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through coastal hills and to the Santa Lucía hermitage, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Alcalà De Xivert is defined by rugged coastal paths, Mediterranean forests, and historical landmarks like Xivert Castle. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Alcalà De Xivert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 590 reviews. More than 3400 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalà De Xivert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: There are over 160 hiking trails around Alcalà De Xivert, offering a wide range of experiences from coastal walks to mountain ascents within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The terrain in Alcalà De Xivert is incredibly diverse, characterized by the Sierra de Irta Natural Park where mountains meet the sea. You'll find rugged coastal paths, steep cliffs, secluded coves, and dense Mediterranean vegetation including pine and holm oak forests. Some routes also traverse plains and lead to historical sites on hills.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: Yes, Alcalà De Xivert offers a variety of routes suitable for families. Many of the easier coastal paths and routes exploring the lower sections of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park are great for all ages. There are 74 easy routes available, providing gentle options for a family outing.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover while hiking?
A: Hiking in Alcalà De Xivert often combines natural beauty with historical sites. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/244763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Xivert&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast. Another popular spot is the Santa Lucía hermitage, situated on Monte de San Benet, providing spectacular vantage points over the coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/664484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of les Campanilles&lt;/a&gt; also offers great views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Alcalà De Xivert are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878704463/circular-tour-to-xivert-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Tour to Xivert Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the historic castle and back. Another scenic loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;White Cove – Irta Lighthouse loop from Alcossebre&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The best time for hiking in Alcalà De Xivert is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, making it ideal for exploring both the coastal and mountainous trails. Summers can be very hot, especially for longer or more exposed routes, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in the Sierra de Irta Natural Park and surrounding areas are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit a particular area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Alcalà De Xivert?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 590 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning contrast between the rugged mountains and the Mediterranean sea, the well-preserved natural environment of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels. The historical landmarks and panoramic viewpoints are also often highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: Are there any coastal trails or routes with sea views?
A: Absolutely. Alcalà De Xivert is renowned for its dramatic coastline. The Sierra de Irta Natural Park features numerous trails where mountains descend directly to the sea, offering breathtaking coastal views, steep cliffs, and access to secluded coves. The 'cliff route' and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1670782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribamar Cove – Playa del Moro loop from Cala de Ribamar&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples of routes that showcase the region's captivating shoreline.

Q: How accessible are the trailheads by public transport or car parking?
A: While some trailheads, especially those closer to Alcossebre, may have limited public transport options, many are accessible by car. Parking is often available near popular starting points, particularly for routes leading to attractions like Xivert Castle or coastal access points. For more remote trails within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, driving is generally the most convenient option.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Alcalà De Xivert offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878681969/natural-park-of-serra-d-irta-circular-from-alcocebre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Serra d'Irta — Circular from Alcocebre&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 28 km path that explores the rugged landscape of the Sierra de Irta, featuring significant elevation changes and varied terrain. There are 10 difficult routes in total for those looking for a strenuous adventure.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see in the Sierra de Irta Natural Park?
A: The Sierra de Irta Natural Park is a highlight of the region, offering a unique blend of natural features. You'll encounter dense Mediterranean vegetation, including pine trees and holm oaks, alongside aromatic shrubs. The park is famous for its dramatic coastline, with mountains plunging into the sea, creating steep cliffs, hidden coves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5508453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribamar Cove&lt;/a&gt;, and pristine beaches. It's also home to diverse wildlife, making it excellent for nature observation.
tips: The top of Les Campanilles at 520 meters above sea level is one of the highest points within the Parc Natural de la Serra d'Irta. From here you will have amazing panoramic views in all directions. It can be reached by several trails of varying technical difficulty depending on the choice of each one.

Just a couple of km from the center of Alcocebre is the lighthouse that offers wonderful views. It is advisable to get here along the promenade, a very picturesque path along the coast, very well cared for, in excellent condition and without any difficulty. An ideal place to visit with the whole family!

waypoints.name: Alcossebre Lighthouse (Faro de Alcossebre) – Cala Mundina
